adv. S. सङ्केति from सम with, गम to go. Collectively; conjointly; in a body; in company with; hand in hand; side by side ( sāth ). 1. Ek nārī kā mailā raṅg, lagī rahe woh pī ke saṅg; Ujyāle meṅ saṅg birāje, aṅdhyāre meṅ chhoṛ-ke bhāje . Riddle. Ans. Parchhāīṅ . shade. A female of a sooty hue abides with her love true, When in the light she shines so bright, at night she 's lost to view.

n. m. 1. Association; union ( sāth ). Bālū kī bhīt, ochhe kā saṅg; paturiā kī priti, titlī kā raṅg . Prov. A wall of sand is the base man's company, the harlot's love the fading hue of the butterfly. 2. Caravan ( qāflā ). Tumhārā kahāṅ kā saṅg hai. saṅg jānā , v. n. To go with; accompany. saṅg chhoṛnā , v. n. To leave one's company. Gorī! tere saṅg māṅ gaī umaryā bīt, Ib chālī saṅg chhoṛ-kar, yoh nā rīt prīt! Rus. To the soul. O fair one! all my life I've constant passed with you, To leave me now is not the part of lover true. saṅg sonā , v. n. 1. To sleep with. 2. ( saṅg rahnā ) To co-habit with. saṅg soī to lāj kyā? Prov. Together slept no shame is left. saṅg lag lenā , v. n. See sāth ho lenā . To follow one. saṅg lenā , v. n. To take into one's company. Sagar sakhī ne ab saṅg le-kar chāk pūjne jāūṅgī, Banṛe ne ghoṛī chaṛhāūṅgī! Rus. Maṛ. song. To worship of the potter's wheel with all…

ety: Prk. सण्मिच्छरो; S. शनैश्र्चरः or शनैस्+चरः
def: s.m. (lit. 'slow-going'), The planet Saturn; — Saturday; (met.) ill-luck; poverty, penury; dirt; dirty clothes; a dirty, ill-looking fellow; a miser; a glutton
more: n. m. S. शनैश्चर 1. Saturn. Iskī āṅkhoṅ meṅ sanīchar hai . 2. Saturday (Black Monday). 1. Āj jāne kaisā sanīchar āyā thā ki ab-tak chain nahīṅ milā . 2. Jummā khel khilāve, sanīchar pakaṛ bulāve . Native School boys. Each Friday shows such sport and play, to school taken off on Saturday. 3. Penury; poverty. Jumma chhoṛ sanīchar nahāë uskā sanīchar kabhī na jāë . Prov. Who Friday skips and bathes on Saturday, from him hard Poverty will never keep away. (Friday is the day appointed in the Hadis for Mahommedans to bathe in). 4. A dirty, ill-looking fellow. 5. Dirt; dirty clothes. Āj kapṛe badle, sanīchar utār ḍālā . 6. A miser. 7. A great eater; a glutton. sanīchar ānā , v. n. To come under the influence of Saturn or one's evil star. Tumhāre sir sāṛhe-sāt baras kā sanīchar āyā hai .
subs: (kisī-ke sir), The evil influence of Saturn to come upon (the head of anyone); to come under the influence of an evil star, to suffer misfortune or ill-luck.
wety: Inherited from Middle Hindi سنیچر (sanīcar) (c. 1695), from Prakrit 𑀲𑀡𑀺𑀘𑁆𑀘𑀭 (saṇiccara), likely from Sanskrit *शनैश्चर (*śanaiścara), from शनि (śani) + चर (cara). Cognate with Awadhi and Bhojpuri सनीचर (sanīcar), Punjabi ਛਨਿੱਛਰ /چَھنِچَّھر (chanicchar), Sindhi ڇَن٘ڇَرُ (chañcharu).

Joṛe banat kā ghoṛā lā! ghoṛe banat kā chābuk lā! Itnā de to bahan bulā, nāhīṅ apne ghar bhalī . Hin. Girls' song. If you, my brother Sānjha, should your sister call, I nothing ask of you but fifty suits in all, Of wraps, and boddices seven score and ten or so, Fine lace and handsome rings, and bells too for my toe, A horse fit for my spouse, smart whip on horse to lay, All these give me, then for your sister send you may, If not, in mine own house as well I 'd rather stay. 2. more: n. m. A precis ; syllabus (اجمال 1, 2.). saṅkshep k. , v. a. To summarize; sum up; epitomize.
subs: (-kā, or -ko), To condense, to abridge, to epitomize, make a summary (of), &c.
saṅkshep yah or saṅkshep yah hai
